what_it_does: Records a set of existing conventions already used by Tim so that contributors and agents do not introduce redundant or conflicting patterns.
observations: Frontmatter schema includes id, type, created_date, updated_date, processing_status with a company-repo variant using type/module/aliases/tags/governs/relates-to/status
Links should use aliases rather than filenames
Em-dash ' — ' is the preferred aesthetic separator in titles/aliases
.base views are grouped by a status field with formula columns as the board-rendering idiom
Spaces/ is used as a top-level grouping structure
Canvas architecture maps are kept current by agents
standouts: Concrete, person-attached convention list rather than an abstract standard
Mentions a company-repo variant of frontmatter schema
concerns: No rationale or examples are provided for why each convention was chosen
Relies on external knowledge of who Tim is and what tools '.base', 'Spaces/', and 'Canvas' refer to
notes: A quick reference of Tim's current note, link, dashboard, and canvas conventions meant to be reused rather than replaced.
questions: Who is Tim and what role do they play?
What tool or platform uses '.base' views?
What does 'company-repo variant' specifically refer to?
How are Canvas architecture maps kept current by agents?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the content: it lists conventions to avoid reinvention.
intention_signals: To protect an established personal/team working style from being overwritten by new conventions.
contribution: Provides a concise inventory of existing conventions that a fused system would keep to remain compatible with Tim's note names, frontmatter, aliases, dashboards, and canvas maps.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item enumerating Tim's existing note-naming, frontmatter, linking, separator, dashboard, spaces, and canvas conventions to prevent reinvention.
intention_for_embedding: To document conventions already in use so that future work aligns with them rather than creating alternatives.
